name: claude
attack: STRONGEST COMPETITOR: codex ("The Loom on Trial"). Its most likely failure is CHOICE-PARALYSIS AT THE DOOR — the entry experience is six named examination passes (Origin, Enrollment, Adversary, Plan, Ground Truth, Contract) presented to a cold visitor in a dark chamber. That is a museum kiosk, not an Awwwards Site-of-the-Year. Awwwards juries and real visitors give a site ~10 seconds to deliver an emotional hook; codex spends those seconds asking the visitor to make an informed taxonomy decision about a story they don't know yet. The proposal explicitly says "no conventional long page" and measures progress as "evidence examined" — which is intellectually honest but kills the one thing every SOTY winner has: an authored, escalating first act that the visitor cannot get wrong. The forensic-examiner frame also front-loads its coldest material (ledgers, claim/evidence/correction cards, JSON/CSV export) and defers its only emotional payoff (the tear-and-reweave) behind a pass the visitor may never choose. Secondary compounding failure: XState owning EVERY transition including camera stations is architecturally sound but produces exactly the stiff, station-to-station "PowerPoint in 3D" motion language that juries score down — deterministic camera hops between fixed stations read as constraint, not craft. Codex solved the E2E blade brilliantly and lost the aesthetic blade doing it: it is the most testable proposal and the least likely to be beautiful. (Grok and gemini fail the OTHER blade outright: grok's ferrofluid grab-and-collide and gemini's drag-to-resplice-in-3D-space both make free-form physics interactions LOAD-BEARING for narrative progression — their own risk sections concede the functional blade fails. They are not the strongest competitors; they are the disqualified ones.)
e2e_resolution: THE RESOLUTION: WebGL is PAINT, never TRUTH. One rule, enforced architecturally: no interaction contract may live in the canvas. Concretely, five layers. (1) SINGLE STATE STORE AS SOURCE OF TRUTH: all app state (chapter, scrub position, panel open/closed, filters, stalemate-unlock, selection) lives in a Zustand/XState store, serialized to the URL (?ch=3&scrub=0.42&panels=a,b&sel=hash:9f2e). The WebGL scene is a pure render function of that store: scene = f(state, t). Playwright never asserts pixels for function — it asserts store/URL/DOM state. Every scene is reachable by deep link, so tests jump directly to any state without replaying animation. (2) SEMANTIC DOM TWIN: every knot, thread, chip, scrubber, panel, and export exists as real HTML — either a visible overlay (record readers, D3 scrubber, filter chips as actual <button data-testid>) or a positioned invisible hit-proxy div synced to the projected screen position of its 3D counterpart (an accessibility overlay, the same pattern used for canvas a11y). Click the proxy, store updates, shader reacts. Playwright clicks DOM; the canvas is decoration. This also delivers screen-reader/keyboard access for free: the DOM twin is a navigable document. (3) TEST MODE THAT PINS ALL NONDETERMINISM: a ?e2e=1 flag that (a) seeds every RNG (mulberry32 with fixed seed — no Math.random anywhere in interactive paths), (b) replaces requestAnimationFrame time with a stepped virtual clock (advanceTime(ms) exposed on window), (c) sets physics to converged rest-state instantly (verlet solver runs to fixpoint or loads a baked settle), (d) disables the visitor-thread and any ambient motion, (e) forces prefers-reduced-motion timing. Result: byte-identical state after identical inputs, every run, every machine. (4) INTERACTION MANIFEST + GENERATED TESTS: build step walks the Clotho graph and the component tree, emits a JSON manifest of every interactive element (testid, action, expected state delta, deep-link). Playwright specs are GENERATED from the manifest — "test EVERY interactive element" becomes a coverage assertion (manifest count === tested count), not a hope. New node in the graph → new test, automatically. (5) SPLIT THE BLADES EXPLICITLY: functional blade = generated Playwright against DOM/store in e2e-mode (deterministic, CI-green). Aesthetic blade = separate visual-regression snapshots at pinned virtual-clock timestamps with pinned seed and pinned GPU baseline (or Storybook shader states), plus the human Eye. Physics beauty is judged on captured frames; function is judged on state. The scroll-lock stalemate specifically: unlock condition is 'both panel-opened flags true in store' (two DOM clicks), asserted directly — zero ambiguity, plus a 5s skip affordance that is itself a tested DOM button. Nothing about this diminishes the visuals: shaders, springs, and unraveling run full-fat in production; the test build just pins their inputs.
verdict: VERDICT: A named synthesis — "THE LOOM THAT WEAVES ITSELF, ON TRIAL": claude's chassis (the authored five-shed scroll cinematic into free-exploration tapestry, the shuttle, the unraveling as emotional peak, DOM-owned interaction contracts) hardened with codex's two best organs transplanted in: (a) the XState/semantic-command architecture and generated-manifest testing as the functional blade's enforcement mechanism, and (b) the Evidence Ledger as the persistent 2D truth-mirror with permanent URLs and exports. Claude's proposal is the only one of the four that wins BOTH blades by design: it has the authored escalating narrative and signature moments (unraveling, pull-a-thread, shuttle) that win Awwwards juries, AND it already committed to the correct testability doctrine — all interaction in semantic DOM, WebGL as presentation, URL-driven deterministic state, physics off-main-thread, full reduced-motion mode. Its one honest weakness (the stalemate scroll-lock) is fixed by adopting codex's discipline: the lock becomes an explicit state-machine transition with a visible tension-gauge, a tested 5s skip button, and unlock asserted from the store. Grok and gemini contribute flavor only (thermal/friction shader language for adversarial edges is worth stealing for the supersedes/stalemate treatments) but both are structurally disqualified: they weld narrative progression to non-deterministic physics gestures, which fails the functional blade no matter the mitigation. The synthesis maximizes beauty (cinematic authored arc + one dataset/two modes honesty), is provably testable (generated manifest + pinned-clock e2e mode + DOM twin), accessible (semantic document under the shader, reduced-motion reading flow), and performant (instanced tubes, worker physics, LOD, LCP poster frame).

name: codex
attack: The strongest competitor is Claude’s “The Loom That Weaves Itself,” and its most likely failure is the mandatory STALEMATE scroll lock. It attempts to infer engagement from whether two panels were “read,” but browsers can only deterministically observe explicit actions—not comprehension. Unlocking from dwell time or viewport exposure will flake; unlocking merely from panel-open events makes the ritual superficial; and withholding normal scrolling risks keyboard and screen-reader traps, mobile overscroll bugs, and immediate user abandonment. A timed skip mitigates entrapment but undercuts the claimed emotional necessity. The confrontation should visibly hold tension while offering explicit, semantic “Examine builder,” “Examine certifier,” and “Continue/skip” controls; it must never confiscate scrolling.
e2e_resolution:
Make WebGL a renderer of application state, never the owner of interaction or progression. Implement one XState machine and typed command registry for every action—SELECT_RECORD, OPEN_EVIDENCE, ACK_POSITION, PULL_THREAD, SET_FILTER, SET_TIME, EXPORT, CHANGE_STATION, SKIP, and RESET. DOM/SVG overlays receive all pointer, touch, and keyboard input; the canvas is aria-hidden and has pointer-events disabled. Projected knots use real buttons, projected threads use transparent SVG strokes, and pulling uses a DOM drag surface that dispatches quantized commands such as PULL_THREAD(id, amount). The Evidence Ledger exposes equivalent controls for every graph record, so no function requires vision, precise dragging, or WebGL. Physics and shaders subscribe to state but cannot decide whether an action succeeded: a tear occurs because the state machine entered REVEALED_CORRECTION, not because a spring happened to cross a floating-point threshold.

At build time, compile the story data and command registry into interaction-manifest.json containing every target ID, valid states, supported modalities, expected URL/state result, and accessibility name. Fail the build if an interactive target lacks a semantic proxy or test case. Generate Playwright tests from that manifest: exercise every command by pointer and keyboard, traverse state-machine branches, assert the URL and ledger state, verify focus order and ARIA output, and hash downloaded JSON/CSV/report content. Add adapter tests that click the projected DOM handles at every fixed camera station; dynamic records are covered from the same generated dataset rather than a hand-written sample.

Provide an E2E adapter—not a separate application—using the same React components, reducer, commands, and assets. Under ?e2e=1, load a versioned fixture, cached layout, fixed viewport/DPR and seed; replace requestAnimationFrame with a manually advanced clock; run springs at a fixed timestep or jump them to authored checkpoints; make camera moves instantaneous when requested; disable adaptive LOD and network variability; and await fonts/workers through explicit readiness signals. Never use sleep-based assertions. Keep a smaller production-renderer smoke suite to ensure the real WebGL subscriber follows the same state transitions.

Accessibility and reduced-motion modes must preserve the complete journey as ordinary document flow with the ledger, transcript, filters, exports, and explicit acknowledgments; no scroll trap, hover-only information, audio-only evidence, or drag-only command is permitted. Performance is bounded with a static LCP poster, deferred canvas hydration, precomputed layout, instancing, adaptive DPR, and simulation limited to the selected neighborhood. Finally, make authored beauty regression-testable: capture golden frames for named camera stations and key shader timestamps in pinned Chromium/SwiftShader, test text occlusion and contrast, and enforce frame-time, LCP, and INP budgets. Human review still judges beauty, while deterministic visual tests prevent composition and motion regressions.
verdict: Choose “The Loom on Trial: Deterministic Evidence Weave,” a synthesis of Codex’s station-based examination, XState command architecture, and Evidence Ledger with Claude’s reverse-topological unraveling and luminous correction scars. The complete suspended loom remains the award-facing spectacle, but visitors move among authored camera stations and pull selected threads through semantic DOM controls rather than navigating an unconstrained physics world; the pivotal tear is a deterministic, choreographed rendering of actual failed assumptions, not a simulation outcome. This preserves the strongest emotional image and graph-native honesty while removing scroll imprisonment, runtime force-layout instability, and inaccessible WebGL picking. Its one state machine, generated interaction manifest, equivalent transcript/ledger, bounded simulation, fixed visual checkpoints, and static fallback make it the only direction that can credibly clear the aesthetic, functional, accessibility, and performance blades together.

name: grok
attack: claude's 'THE LOOM THAT WEAVES ITSELF' is the strongest rival (richest signature unravel + hybrid sheds + one-dataset story/explore), but its single most likely failure is the deliberate STALEMATE scroll-jack (ch.3): interrupting the one universal gesture (scroll) to force dual-panel reads creates an instant 'is this broken?' bounce risk, traps keyboard/screen-reader/reduced-motion users without perfect skip/affordance/URL-unlock, and turns the emotional core into an E2E flake vector if 'both panels read' detection has any timing/visibility nondeterminism—exactly the honest-tension thesis collapsing into a gimmick that jails the visitor and fails the functional blade on its proudest moment.
e2e_resolution: Architectural separation, not cosmetics: (1) WebGL/Three.js/r3f canvas is 100% presentational (instanced tubes, verlet/ferrofluid shaders, camera splines) and never the source of truth—run under a forced test mode (?e2e=1 or env) that freezes RNG (seeded PRNG), pins physics timestep to fixed 1/60, disables springs/verlet/fluid settles, bakes LOD to static, and exposes deterministic camera stations via URL hash/query (e.g. #shed=3&knot=hash&cam=x,y,z&t=0.42). (2) Every interactive contract lives in a parallel semantic DOM layer (Tailwind + real buttons, panels, filters, exports, timeline scrubber, tension-gauge, 'read both sides', pull-thread handles) with stable data-testid, ARIA, focus order, and XState/ store machine; clicks/drags on DOM drive the same actions that WebGL hit-targets mirror (raycast only updates visual hover, never owns state). (3) Playwright/Cypress drives only the DOM/URL layer in animation-disabled/reduced-motion mode (prefers-reduced-motion + test flag skips shaders, pre-completes weave, asserts final state hashes/exports/records); visual/shader regression is a separate non-gating Percy/Chromatic pass. (4) Visitor-thread or fluid residues are ephemeral, seeded, and excluded from assertions; deep-linkable state + content-addressed graph packets make every chapter/E2E path reproducible and a11y-fallback to plain reading flow. This lets free-form beauty exist while EVERY element remains deterministically assertable.
verdict: SYNTHESIS 'LOOM ON TRIAL / CRUCIBLE WEAVE' (codex forensic examination passes + grok ferrofluid stalemate crystallization + claude's scarred-shuttle unravel and DOM-first E2E) is the single best direction: award-tier beauty from living tension-loaded Clotho fabric (warp/weft shaders, pull-thread GPU deformation, gate-failure tear + luminous re-splice scars, first-person crucible collisions) that still derives IA from real graph topology, keeps all controls/exports/ledgers/stations in testable semantic DOM + XState/URL, pins physics for deterministic Playwright, and degrades to accessible static reading—maximizing Awwwards spectacle while provably clearing the TWO-BLADE gate on mid-tier hardware.

name: gemini
attack: Claude's proposal is fatally compromised by its reliance on a deliberate scroll-jack for its 'Stalemate' interaction. Hijacking the user's primary navigation vector and tying it to a bespoke WebGL spring-physics tension model is a catastrophic UX anti-pattern that routinely causes accessibility failures and rage-bounces. By conflating document scroll state with non-deterministic 3D physics, Claude guarantees an inherently flaky state machine that will immediately fail the functional E2E blade, rendering its aesthetic achievements completely unshipable.
e2e_resolution: To pass a deterministic E2E test, the WebGL canvas must be strictly demoted to a pure, passive view-layer that strictly obeys a deterministic Finite State Machine (e.g., XState) living entirely in the React DOM. Every interactive 3D element must be perfectly shadowed by invisible, semantically correct HTML elements (buttons, sliders, anchors) continuously synced to the 3D camera's projection matrix. During Playwright testing, an environment flag forces three overrides: it seeds all RNG and disables physics solvers (replacing spring animations with instant state jumps), pins requestAnimationFrame to discrete deterministic ticks, and tests exclusively against the semantic shadow DOM. This guarantees 100 percent flakeless functional assertions, leaving WebGL visual regression to separate, static snapshot tests.
verdict: Codex is the single best direction, striking the necessary balance between Awwwards-tier visual spectacle and ruthless engineering pragmatism. By abandoning the chaotic physics-driven collisions of Grok and the brittle scroll-jacking of Claude, Codex treats the loom as a forensic inspection machine driven by discrete, testable camera stations and a deterministic Evidence Ledger. This architecture structurally guarantees that every breathtaking shader sequence has a 1-to-1 mapping with an accessible, E2E-testable semantic DOM action, successfully passing the TWO-BLADE gate.
